preamble: The close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the close-mid front unrounded vowel /e/ through processes of vowel fronting and unrounding. This shift is often triggered by assimilation to a following front vowel or palatal consonant.

examples.note: Change from ancient Latin varieties to medieval Spanish, retained in Judeo-Spanish.

sources: Schwarzwald, O. (2008). On two derivational phenomena in Judeo-Spanish. In H. Beinart (Ed.), Moreshet Sepharad: The Sephardi Legacy (pp. 399-422). Magnes Press.
